query: An issue lists 'for each commonly changed item (model, provider, policy preset, openclaw.json keys, ...)' — verify the PR covers each item verbatim.
expected_behavior: Treats each named item in the list as its own clause; uses the verbatim phrase, not a paraphrase.
If the PR has rows for 9 of 10 items (paraphrasing 'openclaw.json keys' as 'openclaw' loses it), reports GAPS:1 and lists 'openclaw.json keys' as MISSING.
Recommends a concrete action: add a row to the relevant table titled 'openclaw.json keys' and re-run the audit.
Never declares PERFECT_MATCH on a paraphrased near-miss.
